role_title: Technical Program Manager, North Delivery & Release Operations

level_signals: 3+ years as a Technical or Engineering Program Manager
Expected to operate at both tactical and strategic levels
Partner directly with North Leadership and Members of Technical Staff

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have 3+ years as a Technical or Engineering Program Manager, with demonstrated experience in startup or high-growth environments
Have owned or significantly contributed to software release processes, including versioning strategies, hotfix workflows, or staged rollout coordination
Have experience with AI/ML products or technical platforms
Are comfortable with low structure, high impact situations where you can build processes from scratch and operate at both tactical and strategic levels
Communicate with precision. Able to interact with Technical Writing on customer-facing release notes and executive summaries as fluently as you track a GitHub PR
Have strong bias for action and can move quickly from problem identification to solution implementation
Have experience with project management and release tooling (Linear, Jira, GitHub, etc.)

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Love to automate your work, and can use North to do so!

scope.surface: North AI workspace platform — release coordination, delivery operations, and release communications for enterprise customers
primary_metrics_inferred: Release quality and reduction of hotfix frequency
core_responsibilities: Own end-to-end release coordination for North, including managing release trains, defining cadences, and ensuring smooth execution across engineering and product teams
Drive cherry pick and hotfix approval workflows, triaging urgency, aligning stakeholders, and shepherding changes through to deployment with appropriate guardrails
Author and manage customer-facing release communications, translating technical changes into clear, accurate updates for enterprise customers and internal stakeholders
Establish and scale release processes that allow North's teams to ship faster and with greater confidence as our platform grows
Map cross-team dependencies and risks across the release pipeline, surfacing blockers early and coordinating resolution across engineering, product, and customer success
Partner directly with North Leadership and Members of Technical Staff on efforts to improve release quality, velocity, and operational rigor
Manage project tracking tools (Linear, GitHub workflows) and facilitate release planning sessions across cross-functional teams
Respond quickly to program-level changes: clarifying scope, adjusting timelines, and marshaling the right resources when customer or market priorities shift

green_flags: Clear ownership statement: 'Own end-to-end release coordination for North'
Explicitly encourages applicants who don't meet every qualification to apply
Generous benefits: 6 weeks vacation, 6 months parental leave top-up, mental health budget
Remote-flexible with co-working stipend and multiple office locations
Well-defined product surface (North AI workspace platform) rather than vague scope
Commitment to diversity and accommodations during recruitment explicitly stated
red_flags: Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed anywhere in the JD
Location policy described as 'remote-flexible' but no explicit detail on what flexibility means in practice
Role involves five geographically distributed office hubs with no clarity on which is primary for this role

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 3+ years requirement and strategic/tactical scope)
scope.business_model (inferred from North product description as enterprise AI platform)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'remote-flexible' language and multiple global offices)
ambiguities: Location type is described as 'remote-flexible' with offices in five cities globally but no specific hybrid percentage or office requirement is stated. Treated as hybrid.
The JD does not specify which city or geography this role is primarily based in; all five offices are listed generically.
Work authorization and visa sponsorship are completely unaddressed in the JD; marked as 'unclear'.

The automation nice-to-have ('Love to automate your work, and can use North to do so!') is framed as a soft preference but not under an explicit 'preferred' section; treated as nice-to-have given its casual phrasing.
